title: myrsine medeciloae
intro: Myrsine medeciloae, also known as the Māori potato or Māori tarata, is a shrub native to New Zealand. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic, antibacterial, and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Myrsine medeciloae include its ability to aid in wound healing, reduce pain and inflammation, and act as an antiseptic to prevent infection. Myrsine medeciloae can be prepared as a herbal infusion, poultice, or tincture, which can be used topically or taken internally under the guidance of a healthcare professional.
